items.headline: Equinix data center permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center building is cleared to construct at 131 Great Oaks Boulevard.

items.headline: Chick-fil-A expansion permitted on Blossom Hill Road
items.summary: Chick-fil-A received a permit for a $809,557 demolition and addition project at 1162 Blossom Hill Road, indicating the chain is expanding its footprint in San José rather than relocating.

items.headline: Congregation Kol Emeth permitted to add glass above trellis
items.summary: The synagogue at 4175 Manuela Avenue received a permit to enclose part of an existing trellis with glass, increasing the building's lot coverage. This is a modest expansion of an established community institution.

items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument approved for Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for a public artwork by artist Mario Chiodo titled Seeds of Peace Monument, to be installed at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The artwork memorializes the trust established September 19, 1993.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget set for FY 2026-27
items.summary: Council took action on the FY 2026-27 budget report and annual assessment for the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District. The district's funding decisions affect property owners in the Eastridge commercial area.

items.headline: Health and Safety Element of General Plan updated
items.summary: Cupertino City Council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the General Plan, which guides city land use and policy through 2040. The specific changes and their effect on residents were not detailed in the agenda.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills the top administrative role responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementing Council policy.

items.headline: City Council to review draft water shortage and master plan
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These plans set policy for how the city manages water supply and responds to drought conditions.
